title: Caythorpe Heath Stirling Memorial
crew_summary: 4 killed (1 survived); pilot Flt Sgt. Joseph John Gordon Nicholson

external_narratives.summary: Memorial record for the crew of Stirling LJ450 at Caythorpe Heath. The aircraft from 1654 HCU was shot down by a Luftwaffe Me410 intruder during a night navigation and bombing exercise on 12 April 1944.
key_details: Shot down by Me410 intruder piloted by Hptm Dietrich Puttfarken (II/KG51)
Night navigation exercise with bombing practice on Bassingham Fen range

external_narratives.summary: Official heritage record for the Short Stirling aircraft crash site and memorial at Caythorpe. Crash site covered by a small copse maintained as a memorial to the crew.
key_details: Crash site copse preserved as memorial

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museums register entry for the memorial to the crew of Short Stirling III LJ450 at Caythorpe, Lincolnshire.
key_details: Four killed, four survived by bailing out

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: Photon OSM — Caythorpe Heath named locality at 53.0271 -0.5480
Photon OSM — Caythorpe village at 53.0250 -0.6033 (2km west)
RAF Wigsley (1654 HCU base) ~30km south — consistent with training flight
location_confidence.verification_notes: INACCURATE. Previous coords (53.249, -0.448) ~25km north near Aubourn with no Caythorpe connection. OSM has named locality 'Caythorpe Heath' at 53.0271, -0.5480 matching memorial ID. RAF Wigsley base ~30km S consistent. Village centroid approximate.
